Reconciliation and Remediation</span></span></a></li></ul></section><section class="Sidebar-listWrapper6Q9_yUrG906C rm-Sidebar-section"><h2 class="Sidebar-headingTRQyOa2pk0gh rm-Sidebar-heading">Axonius API</h2><ul class="Sidebar-list_sidebarLayout3RaX72iQNOEI Sidebar-list3cZWQLaBf9k8 rm-Sidebar-list"><li class="Sidebar-item23D-2Kd61_k3"><a class="Sidebar-link2Dsha-r-GKh2 childless text-wrap rm-Sidebar-link" target="_self" href="/docs/axonius-rest-api"><span class="Sidebar-link-textLuTE1ySm4Kqn"><span class="Sidebar-link-text_label1gCT_uPnx7Gu">Axonius REST API</span></span></a></li></ul></section><section class="Sidebar-listWrapper6Q9_yUrG906C rm-Sidebar-section"><h2 class="Sidebar-headingTRQyOa2pk0gh rm-Sidebar-heading">on-demand-training</h2><ul class="Sidebar-list_sidebarLayout3RaX72iQNOEI Sidebar-list3cZWQLaBf9k8 rm-Sidebar-list"><li class="Sidebar-item23D-2Kd61_k3"><a class="Sidebar-link2Dsha-r-GKh2 childless text-wrap rm-Sidebar-link" class="Sidebar-link-textLuTE1ySm4Kqn"><span class="Sidebar-link-text_label1gCT_uPnx7Gu">Axonius User Training</span></span></a></li><li class="Sidebar-item23D-2Kd61_k3"><a type="button"></button></nav><article class="SuperHubDoc-article3ArTrEavUTKg rm-Article rm-Guides-SuperHub"><header id="content-head"><div class="row clearfix"><div class="col-xs-9"><div class="Breadcrumb rm-Breadcrumb SuperHubDoc-breadcrumb3CiEhI-z7_no"><ol class="Breadcrumb-list3wDDSRTNEBCQ rm-Breadcrumb-list"><li class="Breadcrumb-itemhU0JQW_9l3fK rm-Breadcrumb-item"><span>Enforcement Action Library</span><span class="IconWrapper Icon-wrapper2z2wVIeGsiUy"><span class="Icon3_D2ysxFZ_ll Icon-empty6PuNZVw0joPv Breadcrumb-separator1MLziD-gOLe- rm-Breadcrumb-separator" style="--icon-color:inherit;--icon-size:inherit;--icon-stroke-width:2px"></span></span></li><li class="Breadcrumb-itemhU0JQW_9l3fK rm-Breadcrumb-item"><a href="/docs/notify">Notify</a></li></ol></div><h1>Axonius - Send Email</h1></div></div></header><div class="grid-container-fluid" id="content-container"><section class="content-body grid-75 "><div class="rm-Markdown markdown-body rm-Markdown markdown-body ng-non-bindable"><style>/*! tailwindcss v4.1.17 | MIT License | https://tailwindcss.com */
@layer theme, base, components, utilities;
@layer utilities;
</style><p><strong>Axonius - Send Email</strong> sends an email with the query results to a predefined list of recipients for:</p>
<ul>
<li>Assets returned by the selected query or assets selected on the relevant asset page.</li>
</ul>
<blockquote class="callout callout_info" theme="📘"><span class="callout-icon">📘</span><p>Note</p><p>To use this action, you must toggle on <strong>Send emails</strong> in <strong>Email Settings</strong> and configure the email host and port. For more details, see <strong><a href="/docs/configuring-email-settings">Configuring Email Settings</a></strong>.</p></blockquote>
<div class="readme-tailwind"><p>See <a href="/docs/create-ec-set">Creating Enforcement Sets</a> to learn more about adding Enforcement Actions to Enforcement Sets.</p>
<blockquote class="callout callout_info" theme="📘"><span class="callout-icon">📘</span><p>Note</p><ul>
<li>Not all asset types are supported for all Enforcement Actions.</li>
<li>See Actions supported for <a href="/docs/creating-queries-filters#using-activity-log-adapter-fetch-history-asset-investigation-and-findings-queries-in-enforcement-actions">Activity Logs, Adapters Fetch History, and Asset Investigation modules</a>.</li>
<li>See Actions supported for <a href="https://docs.axonius.com/docs/vulnerabilities#using-aggregated-security-findings-queries-in-enforcement-actions">Aggregated Security Findings</a>.</li>
<li>See Actions supported for <a href="software#using-software-queries-in-enforcement-actions">Software</a>.</li>
</ul></blockquote>
<br/></div>
<h2 class="heading heading-2 header-scroll"><div class="heading-anchor anchor waypoint" id="email-template-description"></div><div class="heading-text">Email Template Description</div><a aria-label="Skip link to Email Template Description" class="heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor" href="#email-template-description"></a></h2>
<p>The Axonius email template includes:</p>
<ul>
<li>The name of the Enforcement Action that triggered the email.</li>
<li>For interactive emails only, displays the configured Response buttons, with a warning that the recipient should click a button within 24 hours.</li>
<li>The Enforcement Set configuration.</li>
<li>The Query results table.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="heading heading-3 header-scroll"><div class="heading-anchor anchor waypoint" id="the-enforcement-set-configuration"></div><div class="heading-text">The Enforcement Set Configuration</div><a aria-label="Skip link to The Enforcement Set Configuration" class="heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor" href="#the-enforcement-set-configuration"></a></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Query Name</strong> - The name of the query used to determine which assets are included in the message.</li>
<li><strong>Scheduling</strong> - When the Enforcement Action is scheduled to run.</li>
<li><strong>Total Runs</strong> - How many times the Enforcement Action has actually run.</li>
<li><strong>Main action</strong> - The name of the main action in the Enforcement Set.</li>
<li><strong>Success action</strong> - The name of the action to run if the main action is successful.</li>
<li><strong>Failure action</strong> - The name of the action to run if the main action fails.</li>
<li><strong>Post action</strong> - The name of the action to run after the main action runs, regardless of success or failure.</li>
<li><strong>Custom Message</strong> - Text of the custom message as provided in the <strong>Custom message</strong> box.</li>
<li><strong>Total run results</strong> - The number of assets returned by the query and included in the email.</li>
<li><strong>Previous run results</strong> - The number of assets returned by the previous run of the Enforcement Set.</li>
<li><strong>Added assets</strong> - Number of assets added, if any, from the previous run.</li>
<li><strong>Subtracted assets</strong> - Number of assets removed, if any, from the previous run.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="heading heading-3 header-scroll"><div class="heading-anchor anchor waypoint" id="the-query-results-table"></div><div class="heading-text">The Query Results Table</div><a aria-label="Skip link to The Query Results Table" class="heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor" href="#the-query-results-table"></a></h3>
<p>The Query Results table included in the email message lists the first query results included in the attached CSV file. The number of rows in the Query Results table is the number configured in the <strong>Number of data rows to include</strong> field. The fields included are selected in the <strong>Add asset fields</strong> section.</p>
<p>Click <strong>See all query results</strong> at the bottom of the Query Results table to see the complete list of query results.</p>
<blockquote class="callout callout_info" theme="📘"><span class="callout-icon">📘</span><p>Note</p><p>Some email clients may add extra spaces in emails sent.</p></blockquote>
<p>If you set <a href="/docs/configuring-email-settings#general-email-configuration"><strong>Compress email attachments</strong></a> on the <strong><a href="/docs/configuring-email-settings">Email Settings</a></strong> page, you can send the CSV files as one compressed file. The system warns you if the compressed file reaches 10 Megabytes.</p>
<p>The following screen is an example of an email message (non-interactive).</p>
<span aria-label="Expand image" class="img lightbox closed" role="button" tabindex="0"><span class="lightbox-inner"><img alt="EmailMessageTemplateNew" class="img  " height="auto" loading="lazy" src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Axonius/ax-docs-pub/refs/heads/main/Images/EmailMessageTemplateNew.png" title="" width="auto"/></span></span>
<blockquote class="callout callout_info" theme="📘"><span class="callout-icon">📘</span><p>Note</p><p>When the Enforcement Action is based on <strong>Adapters Fetch History</strong> assets, the <strong>Adapter Name</strong> column in the Query Results table displays the Adapter name preceded by its icon (as in the <a href="/docs/adapters-fetch-history#viewing-fetch-history-data"><strong>Adapters Fetch History</strong> table</a>).</p></blockquote>
<h2 class="heading heading-2 header-scroll"><div class="heading-anchor anchor waypoint" id="required-fields"></div><div class="heading-text">Required Fields</div><a aria-label="Skip link to Required Fields" class="heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor" href="#required-fields"></a></h2>
<p>These fields must be configured to run the Enforcement Set.</p>
<div class="readme-tailwind"><ul>
<li><strong>Action name</strong> - The name of this Enforcement Action. The system sets a default name. You can change the name.</li>
<li><strong>Configure Dynamic Values</strong> <em>(optional)</em> - Toggle on to enter a Dynamic Value statement. See <a href="https://docs.axonius.com/docs/config-ec-conditions">Creating Enforcement Action Dynamic Value Statements</a> to learn more about Dynamic Value statement syntax.</li>
</ul></div>
<ul>
<li><strong>Recipients</strong> - Add the email address of recipients. Press <strong>Enter</strong> to add multiple addresses.</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="heading heading-2 header-scroll"><div class="heading-anchor anchor waypoint" id="csv-configuration"></div><div class="heading-text">CSV Configuration</div><a aria-label="Skip link to CSV Configuration" class="heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor" href="#csv-configuration"></a></h2>
<p>These options are optional.</p>
<ul>
<li>
<p><strong>Attach CSV to email</strong> - Toggle on to attach the CSV file to the email and set the CSV parameters.</p>
<ul>
<li>
<p><strong>Attach CSV with all query results</strong> - Enable this option to include in the email an attached CSV file with all query results.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Query results CSV file name</strong> - Type your own name for the CSV file instead of the default name set by Axonius. Otherwise, leave empty for the system to use the default name set by Axonius for the CSV file.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Attach CSV of changes only in query results</strong> <em>(Default: Disabled)</em> - Select this option to include in the email an attached CSV file with the changes in the query results (compared to the last run of the Enforcement Set). And if no changes are identified in the results, no CSV is generated. Otherwise, attach a CSV of all query results, regardless if there are changes.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<div class="readme-tailwind"><details class="Accordion"><summary class="Accordion-title"><i class="Accordion-toggleIcon fa fa-regular fa-chevron-right"></i><i class="Accordion-icon fa-duotone fa-solid fa-info-circle" style="color:undefined"></i>Additional CSV Settings</summary><div class="Accordion-content"><ul>
<li><strong>Split by asset entities</strong> - Select to create a CSV file where each asset on a device is shown as a separate row. This separates each asset as the &#x27;expand&#x27; option in the application. It separates each asset by its entity. For example, you will be able to know which values were fetched from each adapter connection. If you do not select this option, all values on a device are in the same cell on the CSV file.</li>
<li><strong>Split by field values</strong>  - Choose field value - For complex fields and lists you can create a CSV file where the values of complex fields and lists are represented as separate rows in the file. From the drop-down box select the value that you want to display in the file, &#x27;Tags&#x27; for instance. Only fields that have been discovered are available. For example, if you export by Installed Software, you will be able to see each installed Software name and its version.</li>
<li><strong>Don&#x27;t split complex objects into columns</strong>  - When selected, complex objects appear in a single column in JSON format. By default, each field in a complex object is split into a separate column in the CSV file.</li>
<li><strong>Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields</strong> *(default: <strong>Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields</strong> field under the <strong>System Settings</strong> section in the <strong><a href="https://docs.axonius.com/docs/configuring-user-interface-settings">UI Settings</a></strong> - Specify a delimiter to separate between values within the same field of an exported CSV file, otherwise the delimiter defined in <strong>Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields</strong> is used.</li>
<li><strong>Maximum rows</strong> <em>(default: 1048500)</em> - Specify the maximum number of rows to be included in the CSV file.  When you set a value here the generated CSV file will include the top x rows, based on the specified values. Otherwise, the generated CSV file will include the default maximum rows, set as 1048500.  (note that this value is the maximum value supported by Excel, setting a higher value generates a file that can&#x27;t be displayed fully or correctly in Excel)</li>
<li><strong>Include associated devices (only for Aggregated Security Findings and Software)</strong> - For Software and Aggregated Security Findings queries. Toggle on this option to include the associated devices with the preferred hostname as a predefined field for each software or Aggregated Security Finding.   When you create a CSV file with associated devices (for Aggregated Security Findings or Software),  if the exported query results are larger than the value set under Maximum rows (or the default value of 1048500), an appropriate notice is displayed at the end of the CSV file.<!-- -->
<ul>
<li><strong>Device fields</strong> -  This option is available for Software and Aggregated Security Findings. Select the device fields to add. By default Preferred Host Name is selected. Click add to select more fields. At least one field must be selected. Once you select fields, you can drag and drop to rearrange in the order that you want them to appear in the CSV file. Click the bin icon to remove a device field.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><strong>Include Associated fetch events (only for Fetch History)</strong> - For Adapter Fetch History queries, select this option to include details of the associated Fetch Events in the CSV file that is created.</li>
<li><strong>Exclude parent complex objects columns</strong> <em>(default: Disabled)</em> - Enable this option to hide the parent field of complex fields in exported files.</li>
</ul></div></details></div>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Send email even if no data is returned in the query</strong> <em>(default: Disabled)</em> - Enable this option to send an email even if the query does not return any results. Otherwise, when there is no data returned in the query, does not send an email.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="heading heading-2 header-scroll"><div class="heading-anchor anchor waypoint" id="additional-fields"></div><div class="heading-text">Additional Fields</div><a aria-label="Skip link to Additional Fields" class="heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor" href="#additional-fields"></a></h2>
<p>These fields are optional.
They relate to the contents of the email itself and not of the the CSV file, which is configured in the CSV tab.</p>
<p>For both Queries based on Query Wizard (such as <strong>Users</strong>, <strong>Devices</strong>) and Queries based on filters (<strong>Activity Logs</strong>, <strong>Adapters Fetch History</strong>, <strong>Asset Investigation</strong>, and <strong>Findings</strong>), you can include data extracted from the query result fields in <a href="#the-query-results-table">the Query Results table</a> in the email body.</p>
<ul>
<li>
<p><strong>Hide adapter icons in table</strong> <em>(default: Disabled)</em> -  Select the checkbox to enable hiding the icons from the Query Results table (above). When the Enforcement Action is based on:</p>
<ul>
<li>Any query except Adapters Fetch History - The <strong>Adapters</strong> column does not appear in the Query Results table.</li>
<li><strong>Adapters Fetch History</strong> query - The <strong>Adapter Name</strong> column appears in the first column of the Query Results table but only shows the adapter name; not the adapter icons.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Add asset fields</strong> - Select the fields to include in the email message.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Add adapter fields to email</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>
<p>The first dropdown automatically shows the same asset type as the one selected for the Enforcement Set query (see in General Settings). You can only choose fields for the email message from this query, and therefore this dropdown is disabled.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>For Queries based on Query Wizard: From the Adapters dropdown, select an adapter. Not relevant for Queries based on filters as they do not have adapters.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>The fields box shows common fields that are preselected according to the query type. You can remove any of these fields by clicking the <strong>X</strong> on the field name tile.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<p>The following screen shows the preselected fields for Devices.</p>
<span aria-label="Expand image" class="img lightbox closed" role="button" tabindex="0"><span class="lightbox-inner"><img alt="EmailTemplateAddAssetFields-PreSelectedFields.png" class="img img-align-center " height="auto" loading="lazy" src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Axonius/ax-docs-pub/refs/heads/main/Images/EmailTemplateAddAssetFields-PreSelectedFields.png" title="" width="600px"/></span></span>
<ul>
<li>
<p>For  Queries based on Query Wizard: To add fields from a different adapter, click <strong>Add fields from a different adapter</strong>. A new field selection row is added. Not relevant for Queries based on filters, as they do not have adapters.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Click the trashcan icon to the right of an asset fields row to delete it.</p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Number of data rows to include</strong> <em>(Default: 10)</em> - Select the number of rows of data to include in the email. Additional rows of data can be viewed in <a href="/docs/view-ec-set-history">Run History</a>. A maximum of 30 rows can be displayed.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Email subject</strong> <em>(Default: Axonius Enforcement triggered)</em> - Specify the email subject. Otherwise, the generated  email subject is &#x27;Axonius Enforcement Triggered&#x27;.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Recipients CC</strong> - Add an email list of recipients to which to send the email as CC.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Recipients BCC</strong> - Add an email list of recipients to which to send the email as BCC.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>From</strong> - Fill in the sender email address, if you want this enforcement action to send an email from a sender other than the <strong>Sender address</strong> configured in <a href="/docs/configuring-email-settings">Email Settings</a> or the default sender address.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Logo</strong> - Upload the custom company logo so that it appears in the header of emails sent by the Enforcement Center. Make sure that the <a href="/docs/configuring-user-interface-settings"><strong>Custom company logo for reports and email</strong> option</a> is enabled. The Axonius logo remains in the email footer.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Add Custom message</strong> - Toggle on to include a customized message in the email.</p>
<ul>
<li>
<p><strong>Custom message (up to 10000 characters)</strong> - Specify the body of the email message. Otherwise, the email message is generated with no body.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Use the <strong>Rich Text</strong> editor with either <strong>Text Plain</strong> or <strong>&lt;/&gt; HTML</strong> mode. See <a href="doc:text-and-html-editor-1">Text and HTML Editor</a> for complete instructions.</p>
<p><span aria-label="Expand image" class="img lightbox closed" role="button" tabindex="0"><span class="lightbox-inner"><img alt="" class="img  " height="auto" loading="lazy" src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Axonius/ax-docs-pub/refs/heads/main/img/enforcement_actions/DynamicValuesRichTextHTML.png" title="" width="auto"/></span></span></p>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Email to include custom message only</strong> <em>(default: Disabled)</em> - Select this option to include  in the email the specified custom message only, and not the Axonius email template. Otherwise, the email includes the specified custom message as well as the Axonius email template.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Create list of predefined responses</strong> - This field is only available when configuring an action within a Workflow. It allows you to embed interactive buttons directly into the email message sent by the Workflow.</p>
<ul>
<li>Enter the text for each response button (e.g., &#x27;Approve&#x27;, &#x27;Reject&#x27;, &#x27;Yes&#x27;, &#x27;No&#x27;, &#x27;Escalate&#x27;).</li>
<li>Click <strong>Add</strong> to add each response to the list.</li>
<li>The buttons appear in the <strong>Your Action is Required</strong> section on the top part of the email message, in the order they are added here. They are clickable for 24 hours from the time the email is sent.</li>
<li>When the recipient clicks a button in the received email:<!-- -->
<ul>
<li>The user gets a message from Axonius: &#x27;Your response was registered. Thank you&#x27;.</li>
<li>The user receives a confirmation response. A user can only send a single response. If the user clicks a response button a second time, they receive a message from Axonius that the reply was already registered. In subsequent times, the user is directed to contact Axoinus support.</li>
<li>The Workflow is immediately triggered to continue along the path corresponding to that selected response.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<h2 class="heading heading-2 header-scroll"><div class="heading-anchor anchor waypoint" id="examples"></div><div class="heading-text">Examples</div><a aria-label="Skip link to Examples" class="heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor" href="#examples"></a></h2>
<h3 class="heading heading-3 header-scroll"><div class="heading-anchor anchor waypoint" id="example-with-query-based-on-query-wizard"></div><div class="heading-text">Example with Query based on Query Wizard</div><a aria-label="Skip link to Example with Query based on Query Wizard" class="heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor" href="#example-with-query-based-on-query-wizard"></a></h3>
<p>For an enforcement set configured to run <strong>Axonius - Send Email</strong> on <strong>Devices</strong> that are noncompliant, this action can be configured to send to each device owner an email with a Query Results table that has specific information on the noncompliant device, such as device manufacturer, device model, and serial number, and steps for remediation, such as to install secure updates.</p>
<h3 class="heading heading-3 header-scroll"><div class="heading-anchor anchor waypoint" id="example-with-query-based-on-filter"></div><div class="heading-text">Example with Query based on Filter</div><a aria-label="Skip link to Example with Query based on Filter" class="heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor" href="#example-with-query-based-on-filter"></a></h3>
<p>For an enforcement set configured to run <strong>Axonius - Send Email</strong> on <strong>Adapters Fetch History</strong> failed adapters, this action can be configured to send an email with a Query Results table that has information (fields) on each adapter that fails.  Recipients of this email can make a decision on how to proceed, based on a quick analysis of the information in the email.</p>
<hr/>
<div class="readme-tailwind"><p>For more details about other enforcement actions available, see <a href="/docs/action-library">Action Library</a>.</p></div></div><div align="center" class="Flex Flex_row " justify="between" style="--flex-gap:var(--md)"><div class="UpdatedAt"><p class="DateLine "><i class="icon icon-watch"></i>Updated<!-- --> <!-- -->13 days ago<!-- --> </p></div></div><hr class="NextStepsDivider"/><nav aria-label="Pagination Controls" class="PaginationControlsjDYuqu8pBMUy rm-Pagination"><a class="PaginationControls-link254uPEbaP92i PaginationControls-link_left1G58BmNHDbVm" aria-label="Previous Page: Axonius - Push System Notification" href="/docs/push-system-notification"><span class="PaginationControls-iconZbaZ3tgyhNmI icon-arrow-left2"></span><div class="PaginationControls-text3qEXHZU00znD PaginationControls-text_left3wBkn4C9R0W_">Axonius - Push System Notification</div></a><a class="PaginationControls-link254uPEbaP92i PaginationControls-link_right5lsfuICZqXYk" aria-label="Next Page: Box - Send CSV" href="/docs/send-csv-to-box"><div class="PaginationControls-text3qEXHZU00znD PaginationControls-text_right3EIfWubgRVVP">Box - Send CSV</div><span class="PaginationControls-iconZbaZ3tgyhNmI icon-arrow-right2"></span></a></nav></section><section class="content-toc grid-25 "></section></div></article></div></main><footer aria-label="Status banner" class="Footer2U8XAPoGhlgO AppFooter rm-Banners"></footer><footer><div class="custom-footer">

</footer><div class="ModalWrapper" id="ChatGPT-modal"></div></div></div></div><div class="ModalWrapper" id="tutorialmodal-root"></div></div></div><div class="ng-non-bindable"><script id="ssr-props" type="application/json">{"sidebars":{},"apiBaseUrl":"/","baseUrl":"/","search":{"appId":"T28YKFATPY","searchApiKey":"","indexName":"readme_search_v2","projectsMeta":[{"modules":{"landing":false,"docs":true,"examples":true,"reference":false,"graphql":false,"changelog":true,"discuss":false,"suggested_edits":false,"custompages":false,"tutorials":false},"id":"688ce1a296b026fdd4f1b4a2","name":"Axonius Asset Cloud","subdomain":"axonius-help-docs","subpath":"","nav_names":{"docs":"Axonius Documentation","reference":"","changelog":"Release Notes","discuss":"","recipes":"","tutorials":""}}],"urlManagerOpts":{"lang":"en","parent":{"subdomain":"axonius-help-group","childrenProjects":["axonius-help-docs"]},"project":{"subdomain":"axonius-help-docs"},"version":"1.0"}},"customBlocks":[{"name":"CSVenforcements","source":"\u003cAccordion title=\"Additional CSV Settings\" icon=\"fa-info-circle\">\n  * **Split by asset entities** - Select to create a CSV file where each asset on a device is shown as a separate row. This separates each asset as the 'expand' option in the application. It separates each asset by its entity. For example, you will be able to know which values were fetched from each adapter connection. If you do not select this option, all values on a device are in the same cell on the CSV file.\n  * **Split by field values**  - Choose field value - For complex fields and lists you can create a CSV file where the values of complex fields and lists are represented as separate rows in the file. From the drop-down box select the value that you want to display in the file, 'Tags' for instance. Only fields that have been discovered are available. For example, if you export by Installed Software, you will be able to see each installed Software name and its version.\n  * **Don't split complex objects into columns**  - When selected, complex objects appear in a single column in JSON format. By default, each field in a complex object is split into a separate column in the CSV file.\n  * **Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields** \\*(default: **Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields** field under the **System Settings** section in the **[UI Settings](https://docs.axonius.com/docs/configuring-user-interface-settings)** - Specify a delimiter to separate between values within the same field of an exported CSV file, otherwise the delimiter defined in **Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields** is used.\n  * **Maximum rows** *(default: 1048500)* - Specify the maximum number of rows to be included in the CSV file.  When you set a value here the generated CSV file will include the top x rows, based on the specified values. Otherwise, the generated CSV file will include the default maximum rows, set as 1048500.  (note that this value is the maximum value supported by Excel, setting a higher value generates a file that can't be displayed fully or correctly in Excel)\n  * **Include associated devices (only for Aggregated Security Findings and Software)** - For Software and Aggregated Security Findings queries. Toggle on this option to include the associated devices with the preferred hostname as a predefined field for each software or Aggregated Security Finding.   When you create a CSV file with associated devices (for Aggregated Security Findings or Software),  if the exported query results are larger than the value set under Maximum rows (or the default value of 1048500), an appropriate notice is displayed at the end of the CSV file.\n    * **Device fields** -  This option is available for Software and Aggregated Security Findings. Select the device fields to add. By default Preferred Host Name is selected. Click add to select more fields. At least one field must be selected. Once you select fields, you can drag and drop to rearrange in the order that you want them to appear in the CSV file. Click the bin icon to remove a device field.\n  * **Include Associated fetch events (only for Fetch History)** - For Adapter Fetch History queries, select this option to include details of the associated Fetch Events in the CSV file that is created.\n  * **Exclude parent complex objects columns** *(default: Disabled)* - Enable this option to hide the parent field of complex fields in exported files.\n\u003c/Accordion>","type":"content","project":"688ce1a296b026fdd4f1b4a2","links":{"project":"/projects/me"},"tag":"CSVenforcements","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:50:28.973Z","usage":{"page_count":17,"project_count":1},"renderable":{"status":true},"uri":"/branches/1.0/custom_blocks/CSVenforcements","branch":{"uri":"/branches/1.0","name":"1.0"}},{"name":"EC_Action_General_Settings","source":"* **Action name** - The name of this Enforcement Action. The system sets a default name. You can change the name.\n* **Configure Dynamic Values** _(optional)_ - Toggle on to enter a Dynamic Value statement. See [Creating Enforcement Action Dynamic Value Statements](https://docs.axonius.com/docs/config-ec-conditions) to learn more about Dynamic Value statement syntax.","type":"content","project":"688ce1a296b026fdd4f1b4a2","links":{"project":"/projects/me"},"tag":"EC_Action_General_Settings","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:50:29.021Z","usage":{"page_count":660,"project_count":1},"renderable":{"status":true},"uri":"/branches/1.0/custom_blocks/EC_Action_General_Settings","branch":{"uri":"/branches/1.0","name":"1.0"}},{"name":"EC_Link_To_Create_EC_Set","source":"See [Creating Enforcement Sets](/docs/create-ec-set) to learn more about adding Enforcement Actions to Enforcement Sets.\n\n\u003cCallout icon=\"📘\" theme=\"info\">\n  Note\n\n  * Not all asset types are supported for all Enforcement Actions.\n  * See Actions supported for [Activity Logs, Adapters Fetch History, and Asset Investigation modules](/docs/creating-queries-filters#using-activity-log-adapter-fetch-history-asset-investigation-and-findings-queries-in-enforcement-actions).\n  * See Actions supported for [Aggregated Security Findings](https://docs.axonius.com/docs/vulnerabilities#using-aggregated-security-findings-queries-in-enforcement-actions).\n  * See Actions supported for [Software](software#using-software-queries-in-enforcement-actions).\n\u003c/Callout>\n\n\u003cbr />","type":"content","project":"688ce1a296b026fdd4f1b4a2","links":{"project":"/projects/me"},"tag":"EC_Link_To_Create_EC_Set","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:50:29.037Z","usage":{"page_count":645,"project_count":1},"renderable":{"status":true},"uri":"/branches/1.0/custom_blocks/EC_Link_To_Create_EC_Set","branch":{"uri":"/branches/1.0","name":"1.0"}},{"name":"ECAction_To_learn_more","source":"For Email","type":"basic","href":{"dash":"https://dash.readme.com/project/axonius-help-docs/v1.0/docs/send-email","hub":"https://docs.axonius.com/axonius-help-docs/docs/send-email","github_url":"https://github.com/Axonius/ax-docs/edit/v1.0/docs/Enforcement Cloud","subdomain":"axonius-help-docs","uri":"/projects/me"},"renderable":{"status":true},"updated_at":"2026-02-26T11:23:30.000Z","uri":"/branches/1.0/guides/send-email"},"meta":{"baseUrl":"/","description":"Axonius - Send Email sends an email with the query results to a predefined list of recipients for:\n\nAssets returned by the selected query or assets selected on the relevant asset page.\n\n📘NoteTo use this action, you must toggle on Send emails in Email Settings and configure the email host and port. …","hidden":false,"image":[],"metaTitle":"Axonius - Send class=\"toc-list\">\u003cli>\u003ca class=\"tocHeader\" href=\"#\">\u003ci class=\"icon icon-text-align-left\">\u003c/i>Table of Contents\u003c/a>\u003c/li>\u003cli class=\"toc-children\">\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\n\u003ca href=\"#email-template-description\">Email Template Description\u003c/a>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\u003ca href=\"#the-enforcement-set-configuration\">The Enforcement Set Configuration\u003c/a>\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003ca href=\"#the-query-results-table\">The Query Results Table\u003c/a>\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003ca href=\"#required-fields\">Required Fields\u003c/a>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003ca href=\"#csv-configuration\">CSV Configuration\u003c/a>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003ca href=\"#additional-fields\">Additional Fields\u003c/a>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003ca href=\"#examples\">Examples\u003c/a>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\u003ca href=\"#example-with-query-based-on-query-wizard\">Example with Query based on Query Wizard\u003c/a>\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003ca href=\"#example-with-query-based-on-filter\">Example with Query based on Filter\u003c/a>\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\u003c/li>\u003c/ul>\u003c/nav>","body":"\u003cstyle>/*! tailwindcss v4.1.17 | MIT License | https://tailwindcss.com */\n@layer theme, base, components, utilities;\n@layer utilities;\n\u003c/style>\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Axonius - Send Email\u003c/strong> sends an email with the query results to a predefined list of recipients for:\u003c/p>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>Assets returned by the selected query or assets selected on the relevant asset page.\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003cblockquote class=\"callout callout_info\" theme=\"📘\">\u003cspan class=\"callout-icon\">📘\u003c/span>\u003cp>Note\u003c/p>\u003cp>To use this action, you must toggle on \u003cstrong>Send emails\u003c/strong> in \u003cstrong>Email Settings\u003c/strong> and configure the email host and port. For more details, see \u003cstrong>\u003ca href=\"/docs/configuring-email-settings\">Configuring Email Settings\u003c/a>\u003c/strong>.\u003c/p>\u003c/blockquote>\n\u003cdiv class=\"readme-tailwind\">\u003cp>See \u003ca href=\"/docs/create-ec-set\">Creating Enforcement Sets\u003c/a> to learn more about adding Enforcement Actions to Enforcement Sets.\u003c/p>\n\u003cblockquote class=\"callout callout_info\" theme=\"📘\">\u003cspan class=\"callout-icon\">📘\u003c/span>\u003cp>Note\u003c/p>\u003cul>\n\u003cli>Not all asset types are supported for all Enforcement Actions.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>See Actions supported for \u003ca href=\"/docs/creating-queries-filters#using-activity-log-adapter-fetch-history-asset-investigation-and-findings-queries-in-enforcement-actions\">Activity Logs, Adapters Fetch History, and Asset Investigation modules\u003c/a>.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>See Actions supported for \u003ca href=\"https://docs.axonius.com/docs/vulnerabilities#using-aggregated-security-findings-queries-in-enforcement-actions\">Aggregated Security Findings\u003c/a>.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>See Actions supported for \u003ca href=\"software#using-software-queries-in-enforcement-actions\">Software\u003c/a>.\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\u003c/blockquote>\n\u003cbr/>\u003c/div>\n\u003ch2 class=\"heading heading-2 header-scroll\">\u003cdiv class=\"heading-anchor anchor waypoint\" id=\"email-template-description\">\u003c/div>\u003cdiv class=\"heading-text\">Email Template Description\u003c/div>\u003ca aria-label=\"Skip link to Email Template Description\" class=\"heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor\" href=\"#email-template-description\">\u003c/a>\u003c/h2>\n\u003cp>The Axonius email template includes:\u003c/p>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>The name of the Enforcement Action that triggered the email.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>For interactive emails only, displays the configured Response buttons, with a warning that the recipient should click a button within 24 hours.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>The Enforcement Set configuration.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>The Query results table.\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003ch3 class=\"heading heading-3 header-scroll\">\u003cdiv class=\"heading-anchor anchor waypoint\" id=\"the-enforcement-set-configuration\">\u003c/div>\u003cdiv class=\"heading-text\">The Enforcement Set Configuration\u003c/div>\u003ca aria-label=\"Skip link to The Enforcement Set Configuration\" class=\"heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor\" href=\"#the-enforcement-set-configuration\">\u003c/a>\u003c/h3>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Query Name\u003c/strong> - The name of the query used to determine which assets are included in the message.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Scheduling\u003c/strong> - When the Enforcement Action is scheduled to run.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Total Runs\u003c/strong> - How many times the Enforcement Action has actually run.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Main action\u003c/strong> - The name of the main action in the Enforcement Set.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Success action\u003c/strong> - The name of the action to run if the main action is successful.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Failure action\u003c/strong> - The name of the action to run if the main action fails.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Post action\u003c/strong> - The name of the action to run after the main action runs, regardless of success or failure.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Custom Message\u003c/strong> - Text of the custom message as provided in the \u003cstrong>Custom message\u003c/strong> box.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Total run results\u003c/strong> - The number of assets returned by the query and included in the email.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Previous run results\u003c/strong> - The number of assets returned by the previous run of the Enforcement Set.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Added assets\u003c/strong> - Number of assets added, if any, from the previous run.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Subtracted assets\u003c/strong> - Number of assets removed, if any, from the previous run.\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003ch3 class=\"heading heading-3 header-scroll\">\u003cdiv class=\"heading-anchor anchor waypoint\" id=\"the-query-results-table\">\u003c/div>\u003cdiv class=\"heading-text\">The Query Results Table\u003c/div>\u003ca aria-label=\"Skip link to The Query Results Table\" class=\"heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor\" href=\"#the-query-results-table\">\u003c/a>\u003c/h3>\n\u003cp>The Query Results table included in the email message lists the first query results included in the attached CSV file. The number of rows in the Query Results table is the number configured in the \u003cstrong>Number of data rows to include\u003c/strong> field. The fields included are selected in the \u003cstrong>Add asset fields\u003c/strong> section.\u003c/p>\n\u003cp>Click \u003cstrong>See all query results\u003c/strong> at the bottom of the Query Results table to see the complete list of query results.\u003c/p>\n\u003cblockquote class=\"callout callout_info\" theme=\"📘\">\u003cspan class=\"callout-icon\">📘\u003c/span>\u003cp>Note\u003c/p>\u003cp>Some email clients may add extra spaces in emails sent.\u003c/p>\u003c/blockquote>\n\u003cp>If you set \u003ca href=\"/docs/configuring-email-settings#general-email-configuration\">\u003cstrong>Compress email attachments\u003c/strong>\u003c/a> on the \u003cstrong>\u003ca href=\"/docs/configuring-email-settings\">Email Settings\u003c/a>\u003c/strong> page, you can send the CSV files as one compressed file. The system warns you if the compressed file reaches 10 Megabytes.\u003c/p>\n\u003cp>The following screen is an example of an email message (non-interactive).\u003c/p>\n\u003cspan aria-label=\"Expand image\" class=\"img lightbox closed\" role=\"button\" tabindex=\"0\">\u003cspan class=\"lightbox-inner\">\u003cimg alt=\"EmailMessageTemplateNew\" class=\"img  \" height=\"auto\" loading=\"lazy\" src=\"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Axonius/ax-docs-pub/refs/heads/main/Images/EmailMessageTemplateNew.png\" title=\"\" width=\"auto\"/>\u003c/span>\u003c/span>\n\u003cblockquote class=\"callout callout_info\" theme=\"📘\">\u003cspan class=\"callout-icon\">📘\u003c/span>\u003cp>Note\u003c/p>\u003cp>When the Enforcement Action is based on \u003cstrong>Adapters Fetch History\u003c/strong> assets, the \u003cstrong>Adapter Name\u003c/strong> column in the Query Results table displays the Adapter name preceded by its icon (as in the \u003ca href=\"/docs/adapters-fetch-history#viewing-fetch-history-data\">\u003cstrong>Adapters Fetch History\u003c/strong> table\u003c/a>).\u003c/p>\u003c/blockquote>\n\u003ch2 class=\"heading heading-2 header-scroll\">\u003cdiv class=\"heading-anchor anchor waypoint\" id=\"required-fields\">\u003c/div>\u003cdiv class=\"heading-text\">Required Fields\u003c/div>\u003ca aria-label=\"Skip link to Required Fields\" class=\"heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor\" href=\"#required-fields\">\u003c/a>\u003c/h2>\n\u003cp>These fields must be configured to run the Enforcement Set.\u003c/p>\n\u003cdiv class=\"readme-tailwind\">\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Action name\u003c/strong> - The name of this Enforcement Action. The system sets a default name. You can change the name.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Configure Dynamic Values\u003c/strong> \u003cem>(optional)\u003c/em> - Toggle on to enter a Dynamic Value statement. See \u003ca href=\"https://docs.axonius.com/docs/config-ec-conditions\">Creating Enforcement Action Dynamic Value Statements\u003c/a> to learn more about Dynamic Value statement syntax.\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\u003c/div>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Recipients\u003c/strong> - Add the email address of recipients. Press \u003cstrong>Enter\u003c/strong> to add multiple addresses.\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003ch2 class=\"heading heading-2 header-scroll\">\u003cdiv class=\"heading-anchor anchor waypoint\" id=\"csv-configuration\">\u003c/div>\u003cdiv class=\"heading-text\">CSV Configuration\u003c/div>\u003ca aria-label=\"Skip link to CSV Configuration\" class=\"heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor\" href=\"#csv-configuration\">\u003c/a>\u003c/h2>\n\u003cp>These options are optional.\u003c/p>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Attach CSV to email\u003c/strong> - Toggle on to attach the CSV file to the email and set the CSV parameters.\u003c/p>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Attach CSV with all query results\u003c/strong> - Enable this option to include in the email an attached CSV file with all query results.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Query results CSV file name\u003c/strong> - Type your own name for the CSV file instead of the default name set by Axonius. Otherwise, leave empty for the system to use the default name set by Axonius for the CSV file.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Attach CSV of changes only in query results\u003c/strong> \u003cem>(Default: Disabled)\u003c/em> - Select this option to include in the email an attached CSV file with the changes in the query results (compared to the last run of the Enforcement Set). And if no changes are identified in the results, no CSV is generated. Otherwise, attach a CSV of all query results, regardless if there are changes.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003cdiv class=\"readme-tailwind\">\u003cdetails class=\"Accordion\">\u003csummary class=\"Accordion-title\">\u003ci class=\"Accordion-toggleIcon fa fa-regular fa-chevron-right\">\u003c/i>\u003ci class=\"Accordion-icon fa-duotone fa-solid fa-info-circle\" style=\"color:undefined\">\u003c/i>Additional CSV Settings\u003c/summary>\u003cdiv class=\"Accordion-content\">\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Split by asset entities\u003c/strong> - Select to create a CSV file where each asset on a device is shown as a separate row. This separates each asset as the &#x27;expand&#x27; option in the application. It separates each asset by its entity. For example, you will be able to know which values were fetched from each adapter connection. If you do not select this option, all values on a device are in the same cell on the CSV file.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Split by field values\u003c/strong>  - Choose field value - For complex fields and lists you can create a CSV file where the values of complex fields and lists are represented as separate rows in the file. From the drop-down box select the value that you want to display in the file, &#x27;Tags&#x27; for instance. Only fields that have been discovered are available. For example, if you export by Installed Software, you will be able to see each installed Software name and its version.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Don&#x27;t split complex objects into columns\u003c/strong>  - When selected, complex objects appear in a single column in JSON format. By default, each field in a complex object is split into a separate column in the CSV file.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields\u003c/strong> *(default: \u003cstrong>Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields\u003c/strong> field under the \u003cstrong>System Settings\u003c/strong> section in the \u003cstrong>\u003ca href=\"https://docs.axonius.com/docs/configuring-user-interface-settings\">UI Settings\u003c/a>\u003c/strong> - Specify a delimiter to separate between values within the same field of an exported CSV file, otherwise the delimiter defined in \u003cstrong>Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields\u003c/strong> is used.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Maximum rows\u003c/strong> \u003cem>(default: 1048500)\u003c/em> - Specify the maximum number of rows to be included in the CSV file.  When you set a value here the generated CSV file will include the top x rows, based on the specified values. Otherwise, the generated CSV file will include the default maximum rows, set as 1048500.  (note that this value is the maximum value supported by Excel, setting a higher value generates a file that can&#x27;t be displayed fully or correctly in Excel)\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Include associated devices (only for Aggregated Security Findings and Software)\u003c/strong> - For Software and Aggregated Security Findings queries. Toggle on this option to include the associated devices with the preferred hostname as a predefined field for each software or Aggregated Security Finding.   When you create a CSV file with associated devices (for Aggregated Security Findings or Software),  if the exported query results are larger than the value set under Maximum rows (or the default value of 1048500), an appropriate notice is displayed at the end of the CSV file.\u003c!-- -->\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Device fields\u003c/strong> -  This option is available for Software and Aggregated Security Findings. Select the device fields to add. By default Preferred Host Name is selected. Click add to select more fields. At least one field must be selected. Once you select fields, you can drag and drop to rearrange in the order that you want them to appear in the CSV file. Click the bin icon to remove a device field.\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Include Associated fetch events (only for Fetch History)\u003c/strong> - For Adapter Fetch History queries, select this option to include details of the associated Fetch Events in the CSV file that is created.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Exclude parent complex objects columns\u003c/strong> \u003cem>(default: Disabled)\u003c/em> - Enable this option to hide the parent field of complex fields in exported files.\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\u003c/div>\u003c/details>\u003c/div>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Send email even if no data is returned in the query\u003c/strong> \u003cem>(default: Disabled)\u003c/em> - Enable this option to send an email even if the query does not return any results. Otherwise, when there is no data returned in the query, does not send an email.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003ch2 class=\"heading heading-2 header-scroll\">\u003cdiv class=\"heading-anchor anchor waypoint\" id=\"additional-fields\">\u003c/div>\u003cdiv class=\"heading-text\">Additional Fields\u003c/div>\u003ca aria-label=\"Skip link to Additional Fields\" class=\"heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor\" href=\"#additional-fields\">\u003c/a>\u003c/h2>\n\u003cp>These fields are optional.\nThey relate to the contents of the email itself and not of the the CSV file, which is configured in the CSV tab.\u003c/p>\n\u003cp>For both Queries based on Query Wizard (such as \u003cstrong>Users\u003c/strong>, \u003cstrong>Devices\u003c/strong>) and Queries based on filters (\u003cstrong>Activity Logs\u003c/strong>, \u003cstrong>Adapters Fetch History\u003c/strong>, \u003cstrong>Asset Investigation\u003c/strong>, and \u003cstrong>Findings\u003c/strong>), you can include data extracted from the query result fields in \u003ca href=\"#the-query-results-table\">the Query Results table\u003c/a> in the email body.\u003c/p>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Hide adapter icons in table\u003c/strong> \u003cem>(default: Disabled)\u003c/em> -  Select the checkbox to enable hiding the icons from the Query Results table (above). When the Enforcement Action is based on:\u003c/p>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>Any query except Adapters Fetch History - The \u003cstrong>Adapters\u003c/strong> column does not appear in the Query Results table.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\u003cstrong>Adapters Fetch History\u003c/strong> query - The \u003cstrong>Adapter Name\u003c/strong> column appears in the first column of the Query Results table but only shows the adapter name; not the adapter icons.\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Add asset fields\u003c/strong> - Select the fields to include in the email message.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Add adapter fields to email\u003c/strong>\u003c/p>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>The first dropdown automatically shows the same asset type as the one selected for the Enforcement Set query (see in General Settings). You can only choose fields for the email message from this query, and therefore this dropdown is disabled.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>For Queries based on Query Wizard: From the Adapters dropdown, select an adapter. Not relevant for Queries based on filters as they do not have adapters.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>The fields box shows common fields that are preselected according to the query type. You can remove any of these fields by clicking the \u003cstrong>X\u003c/strong> on the field name tile.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003cp>The following screen shows the preselected fields for Devices.\u003c/p>\n\u003cspan aria-label=\"Expand image\" class=\"img lightbox closed\" role=\"button\" tabindex=\"0\">\u003cspan class=\"lightbox-inner\">\u003cimg alt=\"EmailTemplateAddAssetFields-PreSelectedFields.png\" class=\"img img-align-center \" height=\"auto\" loading=\"lazy\" src=\"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Axonius/ax-docs-pub/refs/heads/main/Images/EmailTemplateAddAssetFields-PreSelectedFields.png\" title=\"\" width=\"600px\"/>\u003c/span>\u003c/span>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>For  Queries based on Query Wizard: To add fields from a different adapter, click \u003cstrong>Add fields from a different adapter\u003c/strong>. A new field selection row is added. Not relevant for Queries based on filters, as they do not have adapters.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>Click the trashcan icon to the right of an asset fields row to delete it.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Number of data rows to include\u003c/strong> \u003cem>(Default: 10)\u003c/em> - Select the number of rows of data to include in the email. Additional rows of data can be viewed in \u003ca href=\"/docs/view-ec-set-history\">Run History\u003c/a>. A maximum of 30 rows can be displayed.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Email subject\u003c/strong> \u003cem>(Default: Axonius Enforcement triggered)\u003c/em> - Specify the email subject. Otherwise, the generated  email subject is &#x27;Axonius Enforcement Triggered&#x27;.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Recipients CC\u003c/strong> - Add an email list of recipients to which to send the email as CC.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Recipients BCC\u003c/strong> - Add an email list of recipients to which to send the email as BCC.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>From\u003c/strong> - Fill in the sender email address, if you want this enforcement action to send an email from a sender other than the \u003cstrong>Sender address\u003c/strong> configured in \u003ca href=\"/docs/configuring-email-settings\">Email Settings\u003c/a> or the default sender address.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Logo\u003c/strong> - Upload the custom company logo so that it appears in the header of emails sent by the Enforcement Center. Make sure that the \u003ca href=\"/docs/configuring-user-interface-settings\">\u003cstrong>Custom company logo for reports and email\u003c/strong> option\u003c/a> is enabled. The Axonius logo remains in the email footer.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Add Custom message\u003c/strong> - Toggle on to include a customized message in the email.\u003c/p>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Custom message (up to 10000 characters)\u003c/strong> - Specify the body of the email message. Otherwise, the email message is generated with no body.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>Use the \u003cstrong>Rich Text\u003c/strong> editor with either \u003cstrong>Text Plain\u003c/strong> or \u003cstrong>&lt;/&gt; HTML\u003c/strong> mode. See \u003ca href=\"doc:text-and-html-editor-1\">Text and HTML Editor\u003c/a> for complete instructions.\u003c/p>\n\u003cp>\u003cspan aria-label=\"Expand image\" class=\"img lightbox closed\" role=\"button\" tabindex=\"0\">\u003cspan class=\"lightbox-inner\">\u003cimg alt=\"\" class=\"img  \" height=\"auto\" loading=\"lazy\" src=\"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Axonius/ax-docs-pub/refs/heads/main/img/enforcement_actions/DynamicValuesRichTextHTML.png\" title=\"\" width=\"auto\"/>\u003c/span>\u003c/span>\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Email to include custom message only\u003c/strong> \u003cem>(default: Disabled)\u003c/em> - Select this option to include  in the email the specified custom message only, and not the Axonius email template. Otherwise, the email includes the specified custom message as well as the Axonius email template.\u003c/p>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>\n\u003cp>\u003cstrong>Create list of predefined responses\u003c/strong> - This field is only available when configuring an action within a Workflow. It allows you to embed interactive buttons directly into the email message sent by the Workflow.\u003c/p>\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>Enter the text for each response button (e.g., &#x27;Approve&#x27;, &#x27;Reject&#x27;, &#x27;Yes&#x27;, &#x27;No&#x27;, &#x27;Escalate&#x27;).\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>Click \u003cstrong>Add\u003c/strong> to add each response to the list.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>The buttons appear in the \u003cstrong>Your Action is Required\u003c/strong> section on the top part of the email message, in the order they are added here. They are clickable for 24 hours from the time the email is sent.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>When the recipient clicks a button in the received email:\u003c!-- -->\n\u003cul>\n\u003cli>The user gets a message from Axonius: &#x27;Your response was registered. Thank you&#x27;.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>The user receives a confirmation response. A user can only send a single response. If the user clicks a response button a second time, they receive a message from Axonius that the reply was already registered. In subsequent times, the user is directed to contact Axoinus support.\u003c/li>\n\u003cli>The Workflow is immediately triggered to continue along the path corresponding to that selected response.\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003c/li>\n\u003c/ul>\n\u003ch2 class=\"heading heading-2 header-scroll\">\u003cdiv class=\"heading-anchor anchor waypoint\" id=\"examples\">\u003c/div>\u003cdiv class=\"heading-text\">Examples\u003c/div>\u003ca aria-label=\"Skip link to Examples\" class=\"heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor\" href=\"#examples\">\u003c/a>\u003c/h2>\n\u003ch3 class=\"heading heading-3 header-scroll\">\u003cdiv class=\"heading-anchor anchor waypoint\" id=\"example-with-query-based-on-query-wizard\">\u003c/div>\u003cdiv class=\"heading-text\">Example with Query based on Query Wizard\u003c/div>\u003ca aria-label=\"Skip link to Example with Query based on Query Wizard\" class=\"heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor\" href=\"#example-with-query-based-on-query-wizard\">\u003c/a>\u003c/h3>\n\u003cp>For an enforcement set configured to run \u003cstrong>Axonius - Send Email\u003c/strong> on \u003cstrong>Devices\u003c/strong> that are noncompliant, this action can be configured to send to each device owner an email with a Query Results table that has specific information on the noncompliant device, such as device manufacturer, device model, and serial number, and steps for remediation, such as to install secure updates.\u003c/p>\n\u003ch3 class=\"heading heading-3 header-scroll\">\u003cdiv class=\"heading-anchor anchor waypoint\" id=\"example-with-query-based-on-filter\">\u003c/div>\u003cdiv class=\"heading-text\">Example with Query based on Filter\u003c/div>\u003ca aria-label=\"Skip link to Example with Query based on Filter\" class=\"heading-anchor-icon fa fa-regular fa-anchor\" href=\"#example-with-query-based-on-filter\">\u003c/a>\u003c/h3>\n\u003cp>For an enforcement set configured to run \u003cstrong>Axonius - Send Email\u003c/strong> on \u003cstrong>Adapters Fetch History\u003c/strong> failed adapters, this action can be configured to send an email with a Query Results table that has information (fields) on each adapter that fails.  Recipients of this email can make a decision on how to proceed, based on a quick analysis of the information in the email.\u003c/p>\n\u003chr/>\n\u003cdiv class=\"readme-tailwind\">\u003cp>For more details about other enforcement actions available, see \u003ca href=\"/docs/action-library\">Action Library\u003c/a>.\u003c/p>\u003c/div>","css":"/*! tailwindcss v4.1.17 | MIT License | https://tailwindcss.com */\n@layer theme, base, components, utilities;\n@layer utilities;\n"},"opts":{"alwaysThrow":false,"compatibilityMode":false,"copyButtons":true,"correctnewlines":false,"markdownOptions":{"fences":true,"commonmark":true,"gfm":true,"ruleSpaces":false,"listItemIndent":"1","spacedTable":true,"paddedTable":true},"lazyImages":true,"normalize":true,"safeMode":false,"settings":{"position":false},"theme":"light","opts":{"customBlocks":{"CSVenforcements":"\u003cAccordion title=\"Additional CSV Settings\" icon=\"fa-info-circle\">\n  * **Split by asset entities** - Select to create a CSV file where each asset on a device is shown as a separate row. This separates each asset as the 'expand' option in the application. It separates each asset by its entity. For example, you will be able to know which values were fetched from each adapter connection. If you do not select this option, all values on a device are in the same cell on the CSV file.\n  * **Split by field values**  - Choose field value - For complex fields and lists you can create a CSV file where the values of complex fields and lists are represented as separate rows in the file. From the drop-down box select the value that you want to display in the file, 'Tags' for instance. Only fields that have been discovered are available. For example, if you export by Installed Software, you will be able to see each installed Software name and its version.\n  * **Don't split complex objects into columns**  - When selected, complex objects appear in a single column in JSON format. By default, each field in a complex object is split into a separate column in the CSV file.\n  * **Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields** \\*(default: **Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields** field under the **System Settings** section in the **[UI Settings](https://docs.axonius.com/docs/configuring-user-interface-settings)** - Specify a delimiter to separate between values within the same field of an exported CSV file, otherwise the delimiter defined in **Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields** is used.\n  * **Maximum rows** *(default: 1048500)* - Specify the maximum number of rows to be included in the CSV file.  When you set a value here the generated CSV file will include the top x rows, based on the specified values. Otherwise, the generated CSV file will include the default maximum rows, set as 1048500.  (note that this value is the maximum value supported by Excel, setting a higher value generates a file that can't be displayed fully or correctly in Excel)\n  * **Include associated devices (only for Aggregated Security Findings and Software)** - For Software and Aggregated Security Findings queries. Toggle on this option to include the associated devices with the preferred hostname as a predefined field for each software or Aggregated Security Finding.   When you create a CSV file with associated devices (for Aggregated Security Findings or Software),  if the exported query results are larger than the value set under Maximum rows (or the default value of 1048500), an appropriate notice is displayed at the end of the CSV file.\n    * **Device fields** -  This option is available for Software and Aggregated Security Findings. Select the device fields to add. By default Preferred Host Name is selected. Click add to select more fields. At least one field must be selected. Once you select fields, you can drag and drop to rearrange in the order that you want them to appear in the CSV file. Click the bin icon to remove a device field.\n  * **Include Associated fetch events (only for Fetch History)** - For Adapter Fetch History queries, select this option to include details of the associated Fetch Events in the CSV file that is created.\n  * **Exclude parent complex objects columns** *(default: Disabled)* - Enable this option to hide the parent field of complex fields in exported files.\n\u003c/Accordion>","EC_Action_General_Settings":"* **Action name** - The name of this Enforcement Action. The system sets a default name. You can change the name.\n* **Configure Dynamic Values** _(optional)_ - Toggle on to enter a Dynamic Value statement. See [Creating Enforcement Action Dynamic Value Statements](https://docs.axonius.com/docs/config-ec-conditions) to learn more about Dynamic Value statement syntax.","EC_Link_To_Create_EC_Set":"See [Creating Enforcement Sets](/docs/create-ec-set) to learn more about adding Enforcement Actions to Enforcement Sets.\n\n\u003cCallout icon=\"📘\" theme=\"info\">\n  Note\n\n  * Not all asset types are supported for all Enforcement Actions.\n  * See Actions supported for [Activity Logs, Adapters Fetch History, and Asset Investigation modules](/docs/creating-queries-filters#using-activity-log-adapter-fetch-history-asset-investigation-and-findings-queries-in-enforcement-actions).\n  * See Actions supported for [Aggregated Security Findings](https://docs.axonius.com/docs/vulnerabilities#using-aggregated-security-findings-queries-in-enforcement-actions).\n  * See Actions supported for [Software](software#using-software-queries-in-enforcement-actions).\n\u003c/Callout>\n\n\u003cbr />","ECAction_To_learn_more":"For more details about other enforcement actions available, see [Action Library](/docs/action-library)."},"componentTypes":{"CSVenforcements":"content","EC_Action_General_Settings":"content","EC_Link_To_Create_EC_Set":"content","ECAction_To_learn_more":"content"},"resourceID":"/branches/1.0/guides/send-email","resourceType":"page"},"components":{"CSVenforcements":"\u003cAccordion title=\"Additional CSV Settings\" icon=\"fa-info-circle\">\n  * **Split by asset entities** - Select to create a CSV file where each asset on a device is shown as a separate row. This separates each asset as the 'expand' option in the application. It separates each asset by its entity. For example, you will be able to know which values were fetched from each adapter connection. If you do not select this option, all values on a device are in the same cell on the CSV file.\n  * **Split by field values**  - Choose field value - For complex fields and lists you can create a CSV file where the values of complex fields and lists are represented as separate rows in the file. From the drop-down box select the value that you want to display in the file, 'Tags' for instance. Only fields that have been discovered are available. For example, if you export by Installed Software, you will be able to see each installed Software name and its version.\n  * **Don't split complex objects into columns**  - When selected, complex objects appear in a single column in JSON format. By default, each field in a complex object is split into a separate column in the CSV file.\n  * **Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields** \\*(default: **Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields** field under the **System Settings** section in the **[UI Settings](https://docs.axonius.com/docs/configuring-user-interface-settings)** - Specify a delimiter to separate between values within the same field of an exported CSV file, otherwise the delimiter defined in **Export CSV delimiter to use for multi-value fields** is used.\n  * **Maximum rows** *(default: 1048500)* - Specify the maximum number of rows to be included in the CSV file.  When you set a value here the generated CSV file will include the top x rows, based on the specified values. Otherwise, the generated CSV file will include the default maximum rows, set as 1048500.  (note that this value is the maximum value supported by Excel, setting a higher value generates a file that can't be displayed fully or correctly in Excel)\n  * **Include associated devices (only for Aggregated Security Findings and Software)** - For Software and Aggregated Security Findings queries. Toggle on this option to include the associated devices with the preferred hostname as a predefined field for each software or Aggregated Security Finding.   When you create a CSV file with associated devices (for Aggregated Security Findings or Software),  if the exported query results are larger than the value set under Maximum rows (or the default value of 1048500), an appropriate notice is displayed at the end of the CSV file.\n    * **Device fields** -  This option is available for Software and Aggregated Security Findings. Select the device fields to add. By default Preferred Host Name is selected. Click add to select more fields. At least one field must be selected. Once you select fields, you can drag and drop to rearrange in the order that you want them to appear in the CSV file. Click the bin icon to remove a device field.\n  * **Include Associated fetch events (only for Fetch History)** - For Adapter Fetch History queries, select this option to include details of the associated Fetch Events in the CSV file that is created.\n  * **Exclude parent complex objects columns** *(default: Disabled)* - Enable this option to hide the parent field of complex fields in exported files.\n\u003c/Accordion>","EC_Action_General_Settings":"* **Action name** - The name of this Enforcement Action. The system sets a default name. You can change the name.\n* **Configure Dynamic Values** _(optional)_ - Toggle on to enter a Dynamic Value statement. See [Creating Enforcement Action Dynamic Value Statements](https://docs.axonius.com/docs/config-ec-conditions) to learn more about Dynamic Value statement syntax.","EC_Link_To_Create_EC_Set":"See [Creating Enforcement Sets](/docs/create-ec-set) to learn more about adding Enforcement Actions to Enforcement Sets.\n\n\u003cCallout icon=\"📘\" theme=\"info\">\n  Note\n\n  * Not all asset types are supported for all Enforcement Actions.\n  * See Actions supported for [Activity Logs, Adapters Fetch History, and Asset Investigation modules](/docs/creating-queries-filters#using-activity-log-adapter-fetch-history-asset-investigation-and-findings-queries-in-enforcement-actions).\n  * See Actions supported for [Aggregated Security Findings](https://docs.axonius.com/docs/vulnerabilities#using-aggregated-security-findings-queries-in-enforcement-actions).\n  * See Actions supported for [Software](software#using-software-queries-in-enforcement-actions).\n\u003c/Callout>\n\n\u003cbr />","ECAction_To_learn_more":"For more details about other enforcement actions available, see [Action Library](/docs/action-library)."},"baseUrl":"/","terms":[{"term":"parliament","definition":"Owls are generally solitary, but when seen together the group is called a ==========================================================================\n   Axonius / ReadMe\n   ========================================================================== */\n\n/* =============== BASE =============== */\n\n.App,\nbody {\n  font-family: \"Inter\", sans-serif;\n}\n\n@media (max-width: 769px) {\n.hero {\n    display: grid !important;\n    grid-template-columns: auto !important;\n    align-items: center !important;\n    gap: 28px !important;\n}\n.hero-art .hero-img {\n    display: none !important;\n}\n\n  .Header-leftADQdGVqx1wqU,\n  html {\n    overflow-x: hidden !important;\n  }\n}\n\n.Header3zzata9F_ZPQ {\n  box-shadow: none;\n}\n\ntable img {\n  min-width: 25px !important;\n}\n\n/* Buttons */\n.btn {\n  display: inline-block;\n  background: var(--primary);\n  color: #fff;\n  font-weight: 600;\n  padding: 12px 18px;\n  border-radius: 6px;\n  text-decoration: none;\n  box-shadow: none !important;\n}\n\na.btn {\n  color: #fff;\n  /* default white */\n  text-decoration: none;\n  /* optional, if you don’t want underline */\n}\n\na.btn:hover,\na.btn:active {\n  color: #fff !important;\n  /* force white on hover/focus/active */\n}\n\n/* =============== NAV ICONS (image-based) =============== */\n\n.rm-Header-bottom {\n  padding: 10px;\n}\n\n/* Base: make sure ::before actually renders and is visible */\nheader nav a {\n  position: relative;\n}\n\nheader nav a::before {\n  content: \"\" !important;\n  /* force render even if something overrides */\n  display: inline-block;\n  width: 18px;\n  height: 18px;\n  margin-right: 6px;\n  background-repeat: no-repeat;\n  background-position: center;\n  background-size: contain;\n  vertical-align: middle;\n}\n\n/* DARK MODE default: make black art appear white */\n[data-color-mode=\"dark\"] header nav a::before {\n  filter: invert(1) brightness(115%);\n}\n\n@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {\n  [data-color-mode=\"system\"] header nav a::before {\n    filter: invert(1) brightness(115%);\n  }\n}\n\n/* Header link style (light + dark variants) */\n.ThemeContext_line:not(:has(.Header-links-buttons)) .rm-Header-bottom-link {\n  font-size: 1em;\n  height: 100%;\n  margin-left: 0;\n  padding: 8px 10px;\n  font-weight: 500;\n  position: relative;\n  color: #000;\n}\n\n@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {\n  [data-color-mode=\"system\"] .ThemeContext_line:not(:has(.Header-links-buttons)) .rm-Header-bottom-link {\n    color: #fff;\n  }\n}\n\n[data-color-mode=\"dark\"] .ThemeContext_line:not(:has(.Header-links-buttons)) .rm-Header-bottom-link {\n  color: #fff;\n}\n\n/* Active Docs link → force icon orange (including dark) */\nheader nav a[href$=\"/docs/\"].__active::before,\nheader nav a[href$=\"/docs\"].__active::before {\n  filter: none !important;\n  background-image: none !important;\n  -webkit-mask-image: url(\"https://files.readme.io/33afef83fbb810048e002d6001ebffee84f4eb1ae70b9133ba25a56389637b38-Nav_AxoniusDocumentation.svg\");\n  mask-image: url(\"https://files.readme.io/33afef83fbb810048e002d6001ebffee84f4eb1ae70b9133ba25a56389637b38-Nav_AxoniusDocumentation.svg\");\n  -webkit-mask-repeat: no-repeat;\n  mask-repeat: no-repeat;\n  -webkit-mask-position: center;\n  mask-position: center;\n  -webkit-mask-size: contain;\n  mask-size: contain;\n  background-color: #ff6112 !important;\n  /* brand orange */\n}\n\n/* Axonius Documentation — match /docs or /docs/ (absolute OR relative) */\nheader nav a[href$=\"/docs\"],\nheader nav a[href$=\"/docs/\"],\nheader nav a[href*=\"readme.io/docs\"] {\n  /* no image here; image is set on ::before rule below */\n}\n\nheader nav a[href$=\"/docs\"]::before,\nheader nav a[href$=\"/docs/\"]::before,\nheader nav a[href*=\"readme.io/docs\"]::before {\n  --nav-icon: url(\"https://files.readme.io/33afef83fbb810048e002d6001ebffee84f4eb1ae70b9133ba25a56389637b38-Nav_AxoniusDocumentation.svg\");\n  background-image: var(--nav-icon) !important;\n}\n\n/* Support Center — Zendesk or support subdomain */\nheader nav a[href*=\"zendesk.com\"],\nheader nav a[href*=\"support.axonius\"] {}\n\nheader nav a[href*=\"zendesk.com\"]::before,\nheader nav a[href*=\"support.axonius\"]::before {\n  --nav-icon: url(\"https://files.readme.io/13221d07eb15839ad594065fc4ce5591553a120dc558afc1c0b769a39dc81e97-Nav_Support.svg\");\n  background-image: var(--nav-icon) !important;\n}\n\n/* Axonius.com — main marketing site */\nheader nav a[href*=\"://www.axonius.com\"],\nheader nav a[href^=\"/?to=axonius.com\"] {\n  /* extra guard in case routing helpers are used */\n}\n\nheader nav a[href*=\"://www.axonius.com\"]::before,\nheader nav a[href^=\"/?to=axonius.com\"]::before {\n  --nav-icon: url(\"https://files.readme.io/298622abc2c3e29ee5897fcc8550c1a881e705714fd8290a37ea79048e1d1a0d-Nav_Axoniuscom.svg\");\n  background-image: var(--nav-icon) !important;\n}\n\n/* Release Notes — /changelog (absolute OR relative) */\nheader nav a[href$=\"/changelog\"],\nheader nav a[href$=\"/changelog/\"],\nheader nav a[href*=\"readme.io/changelog\"] {}\n\nheader nav a[href$=\"/changelog\"]::before,\nheader nav a[href$=\"/changelog/\"]::before,\nheader nav a[href*=\"readme.io/changelog\"]::before {\n  --nav-icon: url(\"https://files.readme.io/b8f9edc33a418a8625235d1506a6322e5a970bfdd51aec48afab4d3e72c97d28-Nav_ReleaseNotes.svg\");\n  background-image: var(--nav-icon) !important;\n}\n\n/* Hide specific primary-nav items (absolute paths) */\nheader nav a[href=\"/docs\"],\nheader nav a[href=\"/recipes\"],\nheader nav a[href=\"/reference\"],\nheader nav a[href=\"/changelog\"],\nheader nav a[href=\"/discuss\"] {\n  display: none !important;\n}\n\n@media (max-width: 1230px) {\n  .ThemeContext_line:not(:has(.Header-links-buttons)) .rm-Header-bottom-link {\n    font-size: 11px;\n    height: 100%;\n    margin-left: 0;\n    padding: 8px 10px;\n    font-weight: 500;\n    position: relative;\n    color: #000;\n  }\n\n    button.rm-SearchToggle {\n    width: 26px;\n    overflow: hidden;\n  }\n}\n\n\n/* Hide empty TOC and make content fill the space */\n#content-container .content-toc:empty {\n  display: none !important;\n}\n\n/* When TOC is empty, let the body span full width */\n#content-container:has(.content-toc:empty) .content-body {\n  width: 100% !important;\n  max-width: 100% !important;     /* or set a cap:  var(--content-max, 1200px) */\n}\n\n/* In case your grid classes set fixed widths, neutralize them when TOC is empty */\n#content-container:has(.content-toc:empty) .content-body.grid-75 {\n  flex: 1 1 100% !important;\n\n}\n/* =============== HEADER LAYOUT TWEAKS =============== */\n@media (min-width: 1230px) {\n  .Header-leftADQdGVqx1wqU {\n    display: flex;\n    padding: 5px 0 5px 20px;\n    justify-content: center;\n  max-height: 9999px;\n  /* high enough for any section */\n  margin-left: 2px;\n}\n\n/* Heading affordance */\n.rm-Sidebar-heading {\n  cursor: pointer;\n  position: relative;\n  padding-right: 26px;\n  /* more room for bigger icon */\n}\n\n.rm-Sidebar-heading::after {\n  font-family: \"Font Awesome 6 Pro\";\n  font-weight: 400;\n  /* Regular weight */\n  content: \"\\f054\";\n  /* fa-chevron-right */\n  position: absolute;\n  right: 0;\n  top: 50%;\n  transform: translateY(-50%);\n  transition: transform 0.2s ease;\n}\n\n.rm-Sidebar-section.expanded>.rm-Sidebar-heading::after {\n  transform: translateY(-50%) rotate(90deg);\n}\n\n/* ----------------------------------------------\n   Axonius Header Link Hover Styling\n---------------------------------------------- */\n\n.rm-Header-bottom-link {\n  transition: all 0.2s ease-in-out;\n  border-radius: 8px !important;\n  /* match your screenshot’s rounded look */\n  padding: 8px 16px;\n}\n\n/* Default (non-hover) state */\n.rm-Header-bottom-link span,\n.rm-Header-bottom-link svg,\n.rm-Header-bottom-link i {\n\n  transition: color 0.2s ease-in-out;\n}\n\n/* --- override old underline + ensure rounding on hover for accessibility) */\n.rm-Header-bottom-link:hover,\n.rm-Header-bottom-link:focus {\n  background-color: rgba(255, 85, 0, 0.08);\n  /* soft light orange */\n  color: #ff5500;\n  /* orange for text */\n}\n\n.rm-Header-bottom-link:hover span,\n.rm-Header-bottom-link:hover svg,\n.rm-Header-bottom-link:hover i,\n.rm-Header-bottom-link:focus span,\n.rm-Header-bottom-link:focus svg,\n.rm-Header-bottom-link:focus i {\n  color: #ff5500;\n  /* same orange for icons and text */\n}\n\n/* Make the pseudo-element icon orange on hover/focus/active */\nheader nav a.rm-Header-bottom-link:hover::before,\nheader nav a.rm-Header-bottom-link:focus::before,\nheader nav a.rm-Header-bottom-link.__active::before {\n  background-image: none !important;\n
